data.title: Update project docs to reflect PR comment response workflow
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Updates to existing project documentation files to reflect the new /dso:respond-to-pr-comments skill, the comment_response phase in merge-to-main.sh, and the pr_comments.human_in_loop configuration key.
**Why**: Future agents and engineers need accurate documentation of the new capabilities. Without updates, CLAUDE.md Quick Reference will be stale, the merge phase list will be incomplete, and the config key will be undiscoverable.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table — new entry for /dso:respond-to-pr-comments (format: skill invocation + description); CLAUDE.md Architecture section — update merge-to-main.sh phases list to include comment_response phase (after validate, before merge per the implementation); CLAUDE.md Architecture section — document pr_comments.human_in_loop config key and its supersession note (7ac3-71a1); plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md — new pr_comments.human_in_loop entry with default value, behavior description, and supersession note
- OUT: New documentation files; API reference docs; implementation details; KNOWN-ISSUES.md entries (added only if known issues are discovered during implementation)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table includes an entry for /dso:respond-to-pr-comments with a brief description matching its actual behavior
  ← Satisfies: documentation of new skill entry point
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section merge-to-main phases list reflects the addition of the comment_response phase in its correct position
  ← Satisfies: documentation of new merge phase
- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md includes the pr_comments.human_in_loop key with default value (false), behavior description, and a note that it may be superseded by 7ac3-71a1
  ← Satisfies: documentation of new config key

## Considerations

- Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
